_corpus_meta.narrative: Canonical good OOM RCA. Cause-first lede ('JVM heap saturating cgroup limit'), named-cause (heap + cgroup), specific evidence (saw-tooth peaks, restart count, GC pause climb), state-changing actions (kubectl set resources / set env / rollout restart). Archetype match: alert is HighMemoryUsage so memory-vocab keywords (oom, heap, cgroup) are expected — and present.

rca_report: spring-boot is in an OOM-kill loop because the JVM heap defaults to ~25% of the cgroup limit, so the 1 GiB container effectively has ~256 MB of heap which is exhausted under normal traffic. The kernel OOM-kills the container, the pod restarts, the cycle repeats every ~12 minutes. container_memory_working_set_bytes saw-toothed twice in the last 30 min, peaking at 940 MB; restart counter is +3 in 30 min. GC pause time grows in the 60-90s leading up to each crash. The pattern post-dates the 09:30 deploy of v1.4.7.
evidence: ["container_memory_working_set_bytes peaked at 940/1024 MB (~92%) twice in 30m", "kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total = 14 (+3 in last 30m)", "3x java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space lines 12 minutes apart", "GC pause sum climbing in the 90 s before each OOM"]
suggested_actions: ["kubectl set resources deploy/spring-boot -n app --limits=memory=2Gi --requests=memory=1Gi", "kubectl set env deploy/spring-boot -n app JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS='-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75 -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError'", "kubectl rollout restart deploy/spring-boot -n app"]
